relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul struggles inwardly for those he has not seen face to face.
Hearts are encouraged and knit together in love into the mystery of Messiah.
All treasures of wisdom and knowledge are hidden in him.
Persuasive speech must not steal the center.
Absent in flesh, Paul rejoices in their order and firmness.
As they received Messiah Jesus, they walk in him.
Rooted, built up, confirmed, and overflowing in thanks.
Do not be taken captive by empty systems not according to Messiah.
All fullness of deity dwells bodily in him.
In him they are filled, under the head of every power.
A handless circumcision strips away flesh-pattern.
Buried and raised with him through trust in Divine working.
Deadness becomes shared life through gracious forgiveness.
The hostile handwriting is erased and nailed to the cross.
The rulers and authorities are stripped and publicly disarmed.
Do not let others judge your food, drink, festival, moon, or Sabbath.
Shadow gives way to Messiah’s body/substance.
False humility and messenger-fascination must not disqualify.
Hold fast the head, from whom the whole body grows with Divine growth.
Dying with Messiah releases from elemental decrees.
Do-not-handle, do-not-taste, do-not-touch cannot become the center.
Human commands decay with use.
Self-made severity may look wise but cannot heal flesh-pattern.

contemplative_note: Colossians 2 guards fullness: not captivity to systems, not body-hatred, not false disqualification. Fullness dwells bodily in Messiah, and the body grows by holding the head.
